perturbation.original_text: Representatives : any employees, officers, directors, professional advisors or consultants in relation to each Party and any of its Affiliates, who are actively and directly engaged in the Purpose
perturbation.changed_text: Representatives : any employees, officers, directors, professional advisors or consultants in relation to each Party who are actively and directly engaged in the Purpose
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'and any of its Affiliates' from the definition of 'Representatives', this change introduces an in-text contradiction. Clause 4.1(a) allows disclosure of confidential information to the Affiliate’s Representatives, but this change now restricts the definition of 'Representatives' to only those of the main parties, excluding those from Affiliates. Therefore, the section relating to the exception now has an in-text contradiction because disclosure is no longer explicitly allowed for the affiliate's representatives.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Notwithstanding clause 3.1 of this Agreement, the Receiving Party may disclose the Confidential Information : a) to its and its Affiliate’s Representatives , provided always that the Receiving Party procures that such Representatives shall comply with the confidentiality obligations as set out in clause 3 of this Agreement , and the Receiving P arty agrees to be liable for the actions or omissions of such Representatives in relation to the Confidential Information as if they were the actions or omissions of the Receiving Party ; and b) as may be required by an order of any court of competent jurisdic tion or governmental body in which case t he Receiving Party shall, to the extent permitted by law, use reasonable endeavours to provide the Disclosing Party with prompt written notice of any such requirement prior to any disclosure so that the Disclosing P arty may seek a protection order or other appropriate remedy. If no protective order or remedy is obtained by the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party shall disclose only that portion of the Confidential Information that it is legally required to disclose .
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the definition of 'Representatives' by removing 'and any of its Affiliates'. The contradicted text, Clause 4.1(a), explicitly allows disclosure of confidential information to 'its and its Affiliate’s Representatives'. By narrowing the definition of Representatives to exclude those of the Affiliates, the changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text, making it unclear whether disclosure to Affiliate Representatives is still permitted under the agreement. This creates a clear and impactful contradiction related to the scope of permitted disclosures, which falls under Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Confidential Information means all confidential information relating to the Purpose which the Disclosing Party or any of its Affiliates , discloses or makes av ailable, to the Receiving Party or a ny of its Affiliates, before, on or after the Effective Date.
perturbation.changed_text: Confidential Information means all confidential information relating to the Purpose which the Disclosing Party discloses or makes av ailable, to the Receiving Party, before, on or after the Effective Date.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates an in-text contradiction by removing 'or any of its Affiliates' from the definition of 'Confidential Information'. This implies that information disclosed by or to an affiliate is no longer considered confidential under the agreement, directly contradicting clauses that extend confidentiality obligations to affiliates and allowing them to disclose such information. This change would create doubt on whether to disclose information to affiliates or not.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In consideration of the Disclosing Party disclosing Confidential Information to the Receiving Party, the Receiv ing Party undertakes that it shall: a) keep the Confidential Inf ormation secret and confidential, using at least the same degree of care as its uses to protect its own confidential information but no less than a reasonable degree of care ; b) not use or exploit t he Confidential Information in any way except for the Purpose; c) not disclose or make available any Confidential Information in whole or in part to any person except as expressly permitted by and in accordance with this Agreement ; and d) to establish and maint ain adequate security measures to safeguard the Confidential Information from unauthorized access or use.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes 'or any of its Affiliates' from the definition of 'Confidential Information'. The contradicted text states that the receiving party must keep the Confidential Information secret. By removing affiliates from the definition of Confidential Information, it implies information disclosed by affiliates is no longer considered confidential, directly contradicting the obligation to keep Confidential Information secret, thus creating an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The term of this Agreement shall be fo r a period of [three (3) ] years from the Effective Date (the “ Term ”) but may be terminated or extended upon both Parties’ written agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The term of this Agreement shall be fo r a period of [one (1) ] years from the Effective Date (the “ Term ”) but may be terminated or extended upon both Parties’ written agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by shortening the initial term of the agreement from three years to one year. This contradicts the survival clause (10.2) which states that the obligations continue for two years after the *expiration* of the Term. It becomes ambiguous whether the two-year survival period applies to the original three-year term or the newly defined one-year term, causing uncertainty in the length of confidentiality obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The rights and obligations contained in this Agreement shall continue in full force and effect for [two (2) ] years after expiration of the Term or termination of this Agreement .
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the agreement term from three years to one year. The contradicted text states that rights and obligations continue for two years after the 'expiration of the Term'. This creates a direct contradiction because it is now unclear whether the two-year survival period applies to the original three-year term or the new one-year term, leading to inconsistent interpretations of the duration of obligations according to the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: Neither Party shall assign, transfer, subcontract or deal in any other manner with any of its rights and obligations under this Agreement without the prior written agreement of the other Party .
perturbation.changed_text: Neither Party shall assign, transfer, subcontract or deal in any other manner with any of its rights and obligations under this Agreement with the prior written agreement of the other Party .
perturbation.explanation: The change in the clause from without to with causes an in-text contradiction. The original clause states that both parties need to have written agreement, not the changed clause states the same, which is contradicting in the legal world. In this world, the parties don't need an agreement, however in the clause there is a requirement of one.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Assignment and other dealings. Neither Party shall assign, transfer, subcontract or deal in any other manner with any of its rights and obligations under this Agreement with the prior written agreement of the other Party .
perturbation.justification: The original text states that neither party can assign rights without the prior written agreement of the other party. The changed text removes the word 'without', resulting in a clause that states neither party can assign rights *with* the prior written agreement of the other party. This creates a direct contradiction, as it changes the requirement from needing agreement to potentially not needing it. This contradiction is further emphasized by the fact that the changed text and contradicted text are the same. The explanation clearly states that this change leads to contradiction in the legal world, as requiring 'with agreement' is contradicting.

perturbation.original_text: Any notice given to a Party under or in connection with this Agreement shall be in writing and shall be delivered by hand , by pre -paid first class post or by a reputable next -day courier service at its registered office or principal place of business or sent by email to the address specified by the Party in writing .
perturbation.changed_text: Any notice given to a Party under or in connection with this Agreement shall be in writing and shall be delivered by hand , by pre -paid first class post or by a reputable next -day courier service at its registered office or principal place of business.
perturbation.explanation: This creates an in-text contradiction by removing 'or sent by email'. Now emails are not considered as the accepted methods for delivery. There is doubt and a source of conflict whether an email is accepted or not. A party can claim that they didn't receive the message, because it was sent via email.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Notice. Any notice given to a Party under or in connection with this Agreement shall be in writing and shall be delivered by hand , by pre -paid first class post or by a reputable next -day courier service at its registered office or principal place of business. Any notice shall be deemed to have been received : if delivered by hand, at the time the notice is delivered at the proper address ; if sent by pre -paid first class post, on the second business day after posting ; if by next -day courier service, at the time recorded by the courier; or if by email, at the time of transmission. This clause does not apply to the service of any proceedings or other documents in any legal action or, where applicable, any arbitration or other method of dispute resolution.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that notices can be sent by email. The changed text removes 'or sent by email' as an acceptable form of communication. The contradicted text states 'if by email, at the time of transmission' which assumes emails are acceptable. Because the changed text removes email as a delivery method but the contradicted text still references it, this creates a direct conflict, an inconsistency, and therefore a contradiction according to the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.
